mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold, invalidating the labor deterioration channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, which boosts corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which translates into higher short-term equity valuations within the next trading week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, with a hit rate below 45% and a negative information coefficient in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above the 215 index level while the federal funds rate stays low, markets price in aggressive future tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of cases where high inflation coincides with low policy rates, invalidating the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports aggregate demand, which systematically lifts corporate earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.01, or payroll growth fails to correlate with quarterly equity returns with a Spearman rho below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below a 1.5% annualized threshold, corporate revenue projections are downgraded due to weak economic activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields positive forward returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or predictive accuracy does not exceed random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment indicates a synchronized economic upturn that improves short-term market sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample regression tests.
